scoring_method: identical benchmark/questions/scoring rubric as benchmark_sft_base_v1.json, for direct comparison

summary.overall_score: 0 / 28 (0%) on every graded item, in both thinking modes
summary.keyword_match_rate: 1/9 qa items 'matched' (ko_math_1, thinking mode) but purely coincidental — the digit '2' appeared inside a repetitive nonsense loop, not as a real answer
summary.critical_finding: This checkpoint has never seen chat-format tokens (<|bos|>, role markers, <|assistant|>) or the <THINKING> block in a supervised way — it's pure next-token-prediction pretraining. Given a chat-formatted prompt it does NOT answer: it either (a) echoes/mutates the question back as if continuing similar-looking text, or (b) degenerates into short repetition loops (e.g. '보스 : stub' repeated 30+ times, '남은 좌석은 2개입니다' repeated ~15 times). This is the expected, textbook failure mode of an un-tuned base LM and is exactly why the SFT stage exists.
summary.comparison: See benchmark_sft_base_v1.json for the same 28 questions on the SFT'd checkpoint: 0/28->non-zero score on 6/9 qa+open items in no-think mode, 4/17 coding tests passing, one fully-correct coding solution (is_palindrome). SFT clearly moved the model from 'cannot follow instructions at all' to 'attempts to follow instructions, mostly gets content wrong' — a real capability jump even at only 5,000 SFT steps, though still far from reliable.
